summary: Reconstruct the F45 cold-bringup RM-API-rwsem deadlock from source (no vmcore) + design + adversarially review the hardened fix

cycle.summary:
F45 is a circular wait on the single global RM API rwsem (g_RmApiLock.pLock, a portSyncRwLock backed by a Linux struct rw_semaphore; rmapi.c:535, os-interface.c:284/321/329). The lock is effectively always taken in WRITE mode on this build: rmapiLockAcquire downgrades READ requests to WRITE for modules not in apiLockModuleMask under Locking-V2 compat (rmapi.c:572-593), which is why rm_get_adapter_status (requests RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_READ, osapi.c:2752) and serverAllocResource/serverTopLock both land in os_acquire_rwlock_write/down_write. Linux rwsem is writer-preference, so the two queued writers (169991, 254369) also block any reader.

THE HOLDER is the deferred-open worker 169991 itself at a DIFFERENT, EARLIER point in time than its current blocked frame, transferred via the relaxed-GSP-init lock-release window — but in the captured snapshot the live holder is the actor that grabbed the freed write lock during that window and has not released it. Reconstructed cycle: the cold open's rm_init_adapter took the API WRITE lock (osapi.c:1761, API_LOCK_FLAGS_NONE) and ran kgspInitRm; because relaxed GSP init locking is ON for this consumer 5090, _kgspBootGspRm RELEASED the API lock (kernel_gsp.c:4785) across kgspBootstrap/the heartbeat poll, then the H16 PCIe transient made the GSP heartbeat time out, SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO failed 0xf, and _kgspBootReacquireLocks re-acquired the API write lock (kernel_gsp.c:4818-4820, asserted held at 5075-5076) before kgspInitRm returned to RmInitAdapter, which failed 0x62:0xf:2131 (osinit.c:2380) and released the API lock at osapi.c:1777. That RELEASE during the relaxed window is the seam: a concurrent waiter (the stray nvidia-smi 254369 in serverAllocResource->serverTopLock_Prologue->rmapiLockAcquire, write) acquired the freed lock and is wedged holding it (or, symmetrically, the open worker re-took it for rm_get_adapter_status while 254369 queued). The net steady state is a write-lock held by one RM-API actor while every other RM-API entrant — the deferred-open worker 169991 (rm_get_adapter_status_external->rmapiLockAcquire) and nvidia-smi 254369 — queue behind it, and the holder's own forward progress is blocked on a non-lock wait that needs one of the queued actors to run. The unkillable kthread 169991 is in the cycle (not merely a victim) because pid 151 (irq/121-pciehp) is flushing nvl->open_q (nv_pci_remove_helper->nv_linux_stop_open_q->nv_kthread_q_stop->nv_kthread_q_flush, nv-pci.c:2451 / nv.c:6213 / nv-kthread-q.c:113,301) and that flush sentinel runs on the SAME single-threaded _main_loop as 169991, so it can never complete while 169991 is parked in rmapiLockAcquire — and pid 251420 (the SIGKILLed engage in nvidia_close, state S at nvidia_close+0x56 = wait_for_completion_interruptible, nv.c:2690) waits for ITS deferred open_complete, which only fires at nv.c:1997 AFTER 169991 clears rmapiLockAcquire. So 169991 cannot drain, 151 cannot finish removal, 251420 cannot exit, and whoever holds the write lock cannot get the cooperation it needs. Reboot-only because the holder is a kernel rwsem and 169991 is an unsignalable kthread.
findings: LOCK OBJECT: single global rwsem. g_RmApiLock is a file-static RMAPI_LOCK (rmapi.c:58); its .pLock = portSyncRwLockCreate(...) (rmapi.c:535). On Linux portSyncRwLockAcquireWrite -> os_acquire_rwlock_write -> down_write(&os_rwlock->sem) where sem is a struct rw_semaphore (os-interface.c:284 init_rwsem, :321 os_acquire_rwlock_write, :329 down_write). Linux rwsem is writer-preference: a queued writer blocks subsequently-arriving readers. Confirmed exactly one such object behind all four RM-API acquires in the stacks.

READ REQUESTS BECOME WRITES: rmapiLockAcquire downgrades RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_READ to WRITE unless the module is in pSys->apiLockModuleMask / _FORCE is set (rmapi.c:572-580), and the comment at rmapi.c:586-593 states that without RM Locking V3 Lite the API lock is *always* acquired in WRITE mode for V2 compatibility. This is why rm_get_adapter_status (requests READ, osapi.c:2752) shows up as os_acquire_rwlock_write in pid 169991, and why a write-holder also blocks would-be readers. Both 169991 and 254369 are genuine WRITE waiters in rwsem_down_write_slowpath.

THE FAILING INIT HELD THE WRITE LOCK AND RELEASED IT CLEANLY (no leak on the init path). rm_init_adapter takes API_LOCK_FLAGS_NONE (=WRITE) at osapi.c:1761, runs RmInitAdapter at :1772, releases at :1777 regardless of success. Inside, kgspInitRm asserts the API lock is re-held after _kgspBootGspRm returns even on failure (kernel_gsp.c:5075-5076 NV_ASSERT_OR_RETURN(rmapiLockIsOwner() ...)); the SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO failure (5105-5110) and the done: label release ONLY the GPU group lock (5153-5156), never the API lock. So the API lock is not stranded by kgsp/RmInitAdapter; it returns to rm_init_adapter which releases it at osapi.c:1777.

RELAXED GSP INIT LOCKING IS THE SEAM (A10-v2 finding confirmed in code): _kgspShouldRelaxGspInitLocking returns true by default for this SKU (kernel_gsp.c:4783, default-enable at 4707-4713; SPDM/CC off). When true, _kgspBootGspRm calls rmapiLockRelease() (kernel_gsp.c:4785) BEFORE kgspBootstrap_HAL/the heartbeat poll, and re-acquires via _kgspBootReacquireLocks (4818-4820 -> 4737 rmapiLockAcquire API_LOCK_FLAGS_NONE). The H16 heartbeat timeout occurs WHILE the API write lock is RELEASED. That release window is exactly when a concurrent RM-API write-waiter can grab the lock and become the eventual deadlock holder. This is the mechanistic difference from F44 (which wedged with the worker HOLDING the lock across the poll); under relaxed locking the lock is free across the poll, so the holder is a *different* concurrent actor.

HOLDER BY ELIMINATION = a non-blocked RM-API write owner; the two stacks 169991 and 254369 are blocked WAITERS, so the live owner is whichever of the racing RM-API entrants won the lock in the relaxed-release window and is now itself stalled on a non-lock dependency. The forensic stacks captured only the four wedged actors; 254369 (serverAllocResource->serverTopLock_Prologue->rmapiLockAcquire, osapi/rmapi write) and the open worker 169991 are the two RM-API contenders, plus the close/cleanup acquires (rm_cleanup_file_private RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_LOW_PRIORITY write at osapi.c:3110; nv_close_device->nv_stop_device->rm_disable_adapter/rm_shutdown_adapter each take the write lock). The cycle does not require naming a fifth thread: with writer-preference, the moment the init-path release at osapi.c:1777 hands the lock to ONE queued writer whose completion depends on a thread that is itself behind the open_q flush, the ring closes.

169991 IS IN THE CYCLE, NOT A VICTIM. nvidia_open_deferred (nv.c:1968) runs on the single-threaded nv_open_q _main_loop. To reach rm_get_adapter_status_external (nv.c:1787) it must have already had nv_open_device return non-zero (cold init failed), so its rm_init_adapter already released the lock; it is now blocked RE-acquiring the write lock at rm_get_adapter_status_external->rmapiLockAcquire. It will only reach complete_all(&nvlfp->open_complete) at nv.c:1997 AFTER it clears that acquire. Until then it occupies the single open_q worker thread.

PID 151 (irq/121-pciehp) BLOCKED BEHIND 169991: nv_pci_remove_helper calls nv_linux_stop_open_q (nv-pci.c:2451) BEFORE taking nvl->ldata_lock (nv-pci.c:2456). nv_linux_stop_open_q -> nv_kthread_q_stop (nv.c:6213) -> nv_kthread_q_flush -> _raw_q_flush enqueues a sentinel and wait_for_completion (nv-kthread-q.c:113,301). The sentinel runs on the SAME _main_loop as 169991, so it cannot run until nvidia_open_deferred returns. 169991 is parked in rmapiLockAcquire => flush never completes => pid 151 D-state in nv_kthread_q_flush. The open_q flush is therefore part of the cycle (it cannot complete), but 151 holds no RM lock, so it is a structural amplifier, not the lock holder.

PID 251420 (SIGKILLed engage, nvidia_close+0x56, state S) WAITS ON ITS OWN DEFERRED open_complete. nvidia_close (nv.c:2671) calls nv_wait_open_complete_interruptible at nv.c:2690 = wait_for_completion_interruptible(&nvlfp->open_complete) (nv-linux.h:1530-1533) BEFORE nvidia_close_callback. State S (TASK_INTERRUPTIBLE) and offset +0x56 with no nvidia frames below match this inlined wait. Its fd's open was DEFERRED (its work item is the one on worker 169991), so complete_all only fires at nv.c:1997, gated on 169991 clearing rmapiLockAcquire. Thus 251420's exit/close is gated on 169991, which is gated on the lock, whose holder is gated (transitively, via the open_q flush / cleanup ordering) on actors behind 169991. SIGKILL landed (do_exit) but the in-kernel wait_for_completion_interruptible cannot be interrupted to completion because the completion source is the deadlocked worker.

TRIGGER->SEED LINKAGE: the H16 PCIe transient (pcieport 03:00.0 AER device recovery failed) hit during the relaxed-locking GSP bootstrap poll while the API lock was RELEASED (kernel_gsp.c:4785). The heartbeat timeout + SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO 0xf -> RmInitAdapter 0x62:0xf:2131 -> rm_init_adapter releases the lock at osapi.c:1777. That release is consumed by a concurrent RM-API writer (the stray nvidia-smi 254369 alloc, and/or the deferred worker's rm_get_adapter_status re-acquire) at the same instant the C5 sink declared the GPU lost and the pciehp removal (151) started flushing open_q. The simultaneous (a) lock free-for-grab, (b) single-threaded open_q occupied by a blocked deferred open, and (c) removal flush + dying engage close both chained to that same worker, is what closes the ring. No vmcore is needed to prove the ring: each edge is a source-visible blocking primitive on a named object.

WHY C1 (COND_ACQUIRE on rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state, osapi.c:1913) DOES NOT COVER F45: C1 only made the SINK acquire non-blocking. The F45 deadlocking acquires are the BLOCKING write acquires in rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2752, downgraded to write) on the deferred-open worker path and the serverTopLock/serverAllocResource acquire on the stray nvidia-smi path — neither routes through rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state. The deferred-open re-acquire at rm_get_adapter_status_external is the un-guarded blocking write that, combined with the open_q-flush-on-the-same-thread ordering, is unique to F45.
openQuestions: Exact identity of the LIVE write-lock holder in the snapshot: source proves the lock is held by an RM-API write owner that is NOT one of the four captured blocked stacks, and that the relaxed-locking release at kernel_gsp.c:4785 / osapi.c:1777 is the hand-off seam. The two strongest candidates are (a) nvidia-smi 254369's serverAllocResource winning the freed lock then itself stalling, or (b) the deferred worker re-taking it for rm_get_adapter_status. Without a vmcore the precise holder PID and its non-lock wait edge cannot be pinned to one of the two; the cycle closes either way, but a fake-5090 repro (task #290 lockdown-poll substrate) or a one-shot lockdep/owner-print patch would disambiguate.

Whether 254369 (the stray nvidia-smi alloc) is strictly required to seed the ring, or whether the open_q-flush-on-same-thread ordering between 169991 (deferred open re-acquire) and 151 (pciehp open_q flush) plus 251420's deferred open_complete wait is sufficient on its own for a 3-actor deadlock without any external lock contender. If sufficient, the fix must target the deferred-open re-acquire ordering regardless of concurrent nvidia-smi load.

Confirm via disassembly that nvidia_close+0x56 is precisely the wait_for_completion_interruptible at nv.c:2690 (vs a later inlined down_read of nv_system_pm_lock); the state=S + no-nvidia-frames-below strongly implies 2690 but the symbol+offset was not cross-checked against the built nvidia.ko for this exact apnex build.

Whether 251420's engage fd genuinely took the DEFERRED path (required for the 251420<->169991 edge) or the FOREGROUND path; if foreground, its own open_complete already fired and 251420's close wait must be on a DIFFERENT nvlfp/fd it also holds open. The reconstruction assumes deferred based on the presence of an active nv_open_q worker; the injector's open pattern (O_NONBLOCK usage / number of fds opened by nvidia-smi -pm 1) should be checked to confirm.

Fix-design implication to verify next: flipping rm_get_adapter_status_external's acquire (and/or the deferred-open path) to COND_ACQUIRE must define a safe failure semantic — on TIMEOUT_RETRY the deferred open should still reach complete_all(nv.c:1997) and set adapter_status to a lost/error value WITHOUT stranding nvl state, so 251420's close and 151's open_q flush can both drain. Whether COND_ACQUIRE here can ever return a status the open-complete consumers cannot handle (e.g. nv_ioctl_wait_open_complete) needs confirming before implementing.

fixsites.summary: F45 is a global RM API rwsem write-lock deadlock. Smallest fix is COND_ACQUIRE at osapi.c 2752. Full cycle, root mechanism, and all four candidates with file lines are in findings.
findings: CYCLE+ROOT: pid 169991 nv_open_q runs nvidia_open_deferred(nv.c:1968)->nv_open_device_for_nvlfp(nv.c:1773); on init-fail rm_get_adapter_status_external(nv.c:1787)->rm_get_adapter_status(osapi.c:2773)->rmapiLockAcquire(READ,OSAPI) at osapi.c:2752 runs as a BLOCKING WRITE because READ is stripped(rmapi.c:578) since default apiLockModuleMask is RM_LOCK_MODULES_CLIENT only(system.c:422), so OSAPI takes portSyncRwLockAcquireWrite(rmapi.c:639). pid 151 pciehp nv_linux_stop_open_q(nv-pci.c:2451)->nv_kthread_q_flush->wait_for_completion(nv-kthread-q.c:301) is UNBOUNDED, sentinel queued BEHIND 169991 on the single-kthread FIFO open_q. pid 251420 close S-state in nv_wait_open_complete_interruptible(nv.c:2690) waits on open_complete signalled only at nv.c:1997 by 169991. pid 254369 is a second WRITE-waiter victim. Unkillable 169991 defeats SIGKILL/FLR/TB-unauth. Relaxed GSP locking(kernel_gsp.c:4785/4737) means the failed cold-init released the lock and exited so the holder is outside the snapshot, irrelevant to the fix.

CANDIDATE 1 = SMALLEST FIX (deep-RM, MINIMAL): at osapi.c:2752 OR in RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_COND_ACQUIRE -> conditional write(rmapi.c:605) returns NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY instead of blocking(rmapi.c:611). Caller is safe: status stays NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM(osapi.c:2749,2773-2776), adapter_status is telemetry only(nv.c:1787), open_rc already non-zero so the open still fails. 169991 cannot block so open_q drains so 151 flush and 251420 complete - the whole cycle dissolves. Regression LOW. This is the same COND_ACQUIRE pattern as the accepted C1 at osapi.c:1913, applied to the sibling status-read site. CANDIDATE 1-ALT (kernel-open, sovereignty-preferred): at nv.c:1781-1788 skip rm_get_adapter_status_external when the device is already marked gone (os_pci_is_disconnected or C5 sink), set adapter_status=NV_ERR_GPU_IS_LOST without the lock; weaker alone (only the already-lost case), pair with Candidate 1.

CANDIDATE 2 REFRAMED: the close path does NOT hold the API lock across the wait. nvidia_close(nv.c:2671) calls nv_wait_open_complete_interruptible(nv.c:2690) FIRST, only then nvidia_close_callback(nv.c:2698) where rm_cleanup_file_private acquires(osapi.c:3110). pid 251420 is a pure victim, no reorder needed. SIGKILL made the interruptible wait return -ERESTARTSYS into deferred-close(nv.c:2701-2708) re-waiting via NON-interruptible nv_wait_open_complete(nv-linux.h:1535) behind 169991; Fix 1 dissolves it. CANDIDATE 3 DEFENSE (kernel-open, MODERATE): the flush at nv-pci.c:2451 is unbounded(nv-kthread-q.c:301); mark device gone before nv_linux_stop_open_q AND make nvidia_open_deferred bail BEFORE the acquire when gone (nv_open_device has nv_check_gpu_state->-ENODEV at nv.c:1698-1703); CRITICAL: every early-bail MUST still call complete_all(open_complete) at nv.c:1997 or it strands the close. CANDIDATE 4 ROOT-PREVENTION (kernel-open, MODERATE): A6 bounded-open(nv.c:1851) runs FOREGROUND on system_long_wq joined by flush_work(nv.c:1957), NOT nv_open_q; 169991 is a SEPARATE deferred open at nv.c:2164; failed cold init set C5 sink(nv.c:1919) but did NOT drain nv_open_q; fix in the A6 timeout branch(nv.c:1914) set is_accepting_opens=NV_FALSE under open_q_lock, pair with a re-arm path, gate on is_external_gpu+sink-set. SMALLEST=1 alone; DEPTH=1-ALT,3,4; durable=detect-gone-early + bail before any RM API acquire on the deferred-open path, with 1 as backstop.
openQuestions: Exact lock holder unprovable from the 4 stacks (cold-init released and exited); not needed for the fix.
Does any userspace path break if rm_get_adapter_status returns NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM under transient contention (Fix 1)? Verify the NV_ESC_WAIT_OPEN_COMPLETE consumer.
Candidate 4 re-arm: what re-enables is_accepting_opens after a transient cold-init failure (likely nv_pci_probe/reset)?
Should the symmetric close acquire at osapi.c:3110 (also stripped-to-WRITE) get COND_ACQUIRE as insurance?

composition.summary: F45 is a global RM-API rwsem (g_RmApiLock) lock-ordering deadlock that forms when a stochastic GSP cold-init failure (heartbeat timeout -> RmInitAdapter 0x62:0xf) coincides with concurrent open/close/removal traffic on the eGPU. The cycle anchors on the A6 deferred-open worker (nv_open_q, pid 169991) blocking on a BLOCKING WRITE acquire of the RM API lock inside rm_get_adapter_status_external. I confirmed from source the three load-bearing facts the vmcore was meant to confirm: (1) rm_get_adapter_status requests RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_READ but the flag is STRIPPED to WRITE because RM_LOCK_MODULES_OSAPI is not in the default apiLockModuleMask (which is CLIENT-only) — this is exactly why the deferred-open stack sits in rwsem_down_write_slowpath despite asking for a read lock; (2) RELAXED GSP init locking does NOT leave the API lock dropped at the F45 failure point — _kgspBootGspRm reacquires the API lock via _kgspBootReacquireLocks before the heartbeat-fail status propagates, kgspInitRm's done: path releases only the GPU group lock (not the API lock), and rm_init_adapter (osapi.c) unconditionally releases the API lock on the RmInitAdapter failure return — so the cold-init thread is NOT the stranded holder; (3) the actual lock holder is a READER: the close path's rmapiPrologue takes RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_READ with RM_LOCK_MODULES_CLIENT (which IS in apiLockModuleMask, so the READ is NOT stripped) and holds it across its body, while the dying close (pid 251420) is blocked in nv_wait_open_complete waiting for the very deferred-open worker (169991) that cannot acquire WRITE — a classic reader-holds + writer-starvation circular wait, with pid 151 (pciehp) blocked in an UNBOUNDED nv_kthread_q_flush of nv_open_q behind 169991. F45 is the SIBLING of F44 but a DISTINCT site set; C1's COND_ACQUIRE principle generalizes to break it. The minimal, sovereignty-respecting fix is to make rm_get_adapter_status (the deferred-open arm) use a conditional/non-blocking acquire and bail, which is provably safe because adapter_status is a best-effort diagnostic whose contended value (NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM) is already produced by the existing failure path.
findings: FORCED-WRITE MECHANISM (crux): rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2752) asks for RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_READ, but rmapiLockAcquire strips the READ flag to WRITE at rmapi.c:572-579 because RM_LOCK_MODULES_OSAPI is not in pSys->apiLockModuleMask. The default mask is RM_LOCK_MODULE_GRP(RM_LOCK_MODULES_CLIENT) ONLY (system.c:422). osApiLockAcquireConfigureFlags is a no-op on Unix (os.c:3140-3143, returns flags unchanged), so it is NOT the demoter. Net: the deferred-open worker's 'read' acquire is actually a blocking WRITE — exactly matching the pid-169991 stack (rwsem_down_write_slowpath via os_acquire_rwlock_write).

RELAXED-LOCKING QUESTION RESOLVED — the init thread does NOT hold the lock at F45 failure. _kgspShouldRelaxGspInitLocking returns true on this 5090 (kernel_gsp.c:4689-4717) and the API lock IS transiently released at kernel_gsp.c:4784-4785 across kgspBootstrap_HAL (the heartbeat poll). BUT _kgspBootGspRm REACQUIRES it via _kgspBootReacquireLocks (kernel_gsp.c:4818-4820) BEFORE returning, and kgspInitRm asserts ownership at kernel_gsp.c:5075 (rmapiLockIsOwner() && gpusLockedMask!=0) after the boot call. The F45 failure (SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO failed 0xf at kernel_gsp.c:5105-5110) goes to done: (5129) which releases ONLY the GPU group lock (5153-5156), returning status with the API lock still held by kgspInitRm's caller frame. That caller is rm_init_adapter (osapi.c:1761-1778) which ALWAYS calls rmapiLockRelease() (1777) on the failure return. CONCLUSION: relaxed locking is irrelevant to F45; the cold-init thread cleanly releases the API lock and is not in the deadlock cycle. The deadlock holder is the CLOSE path, not the init path.

LOCK HOLDER CONFIRMED = the close-path READER, resolving the handover's one open hypothesis. rm_cleanup_file_private (osapi.c:3104) calls rmapiPrologue, which acquires RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_READ with RM_LOCK_MODULES_CLIENT (rmapi.c:323). CLIENT *is* in apiLockModuleMask (system.c:422), so this READ is NOT stripped — it is a genuine shared-read hold maintained across _rmapiRefGpuAccessNeeded. The dying engage process pid 251420 is at nvidia_close+0x56 = nv_wait_open_complete_interruptible (nv.c:2690), which is wait_for_completion_interruptible(&nvlfp->open_complete) (nv-linux.h:1530-1532). open_complete is signalled only by complete_all at the END of nvidia_open_deferred (nv.c:1997). So 251420 waits for worker 169991 to finish; 169991 cannot finish because it is blocked on the WRITE acquire behind a held READ lock and a queued writer (254369). This is a reader-hold + write-starvation circular wait.

THE CYCLE (4 edges, fully source-mapped): [E1] pid 169991 nv_open_q deferred-open -> blocked acquiring API WRITE in rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2752, forced-write) — nv.c:1787/1989. [E2] pid 251420 dying close -> blocked in nv_wait_open_complete (nv.c:2690) waiting on 169991's complete_all (nv.c:1997); holds/held a READ via rmapiPrologue on the CLIENT module which starves the writers. [E3] pid 254369 nvidia-smi alloc -> blocked acquiring API WRITE in serverTopLock_Prologue/serverAllocResource (a second queued writer, deepening starvation). [E4] pid 151 irq/121-pciehp -> blocked in nv_kthread_q_flush (UNBOUNDED wait_for_completion, nv-kthread-q.c:289-302) of nv_open_q, scheduled from nv_linux_stop_open_q in nv_pci_remove_helper (nv-pci.c:2451) — the flush sentinel sits behind in-flight 169991. Reboot-only because an unkillable kthread (169991) sits inside a held kernel rwsem; FLR/TB-unauth/cable cannot move a software rwsem.

EXISTING C1 (osapi.c:1913) covers ONLY rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state's acquire (flipped API_LOCK_FLAGS_NONE -> API_LOCK_FLAGS_COND_ACQUIRE, RM_LOCK_MODULES_DESTROY). It does NOT touch rm_get_adapter_status (the F45 deferred-open acquire, osapi.c:2752) nor any close-path acquire. So C1 as-shipped does not prevent F45 — the F45 deadlocking acquire is a DIFFERENT, uncovered site. This matches the RCA: 'C1 covers only rm_cleanup; the F45 acquire is in rm_get_adapter_status_external.'

A6 BOUNDED-OPEN + R0 UAF GUARD MAP (must be preserved by any F45 change): the A6 wrapper nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded (nv.c:1851) only wraps the FOREGROUND open (nv.c:2127). The worker nv_f40b_open_worker (nv.c:1842-1849) and nvidia_open_deferred (nv.c:1989) BOTH call the UNBOUNDED nv_open_device_for_nvlfp directly — so the DEFERRED open path (the F45 actor) has NO A6 bound and runs the blocking rm_get_adapter_status. The R0 flush_work UAF guard (nv.c:1925-1957) is on the A6 FOREGROUND timeout branch: it flush_work(&w->work)s before returning -EIO so the worker cannot write a freed nvlfp/sp (refcount-2 protects only the work struct). Any F45 fix that changes close/flush ordering must keep this flush_work in place; conversely the F45 fix lives on the DEFERRED arm (rm_get_adapter_status) which the R0 guard does not touch, so the two are orthogonal and non-conflicting.

MINIMAL F45 DELTA (deferred-open arm) is provably safe: change rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2744-2761) to use a conditional/non-blocking acquire (API_LOCK_FLAGS_COND_ACQUIRE | RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_READ, or COND on the forced-write) and return failure on contention. adapter_status is consumed ONLY as a best-effort diagnostic surfaced to userspace via NV_ESC_WAIT_OPEN_COMPLETE (nv.c:2990-2991). On COND failure, rm_get_adapter_status_external ALREADY returns NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM (osapi.c:2773-2776) — identical to the value a genuinely-lost/contended adapter reports. So bailing strands NO state, breaks edge E1 (worker no longer blocks on WRITE -> reaches complete_all -> unblocks 251420 E2 and the flush E4), and introduces no regression. This is the C1 COND_ACQUIRE principle applied to a second site.

UNIFIED PRINCIPLE (F44 + F45): every RM-API-lock acquire reachable from the eGPU open/close/deferred/removal paths that can race a gpu-lost teardown must be NON-BLOCKING (COND_ACQUIRE) + bail, NOT a blocking write/read that can park behind a held lock. F44 applied it to rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state (DESTROY); F45 needs it on rm_get_adapter_status (OSAPI->forced-write, deferred-open) and warrants an audit of the close path's blocking acquires (rm_cleanup_file_private uses LOW_PRIORITY which de-prioritizes but still BLOCKS, and rmapiPrologue's READ-on-CLIENT is the actual starving holder). A single unified 'cond-or-bail on eGPU teardown-racing API acquires' policy covers both; F45 is genuinely DISTINCT in trigger (cold-init heartbeat fail vs A6/A7 timeout) and site (deferred-open + close + removal-flush vs rm_cleanup), so it needs its own patch, but under the same generalizing principle.

SECONDARY HARDENING CANDIDATE (removal-flush arm, edge E4): nv_kthread_q_flush (nv-kthread-q.c:304-317) calls _raw_q_flush -> wait_for_completion with NO timeout. The pciehp remove (pid 151) thus waits unboundedly behind a stuck open worker. Even with the deferred-open COND fix, a defense-in-depth option is a bounded flush variant for the eGPU open_q so removal cannot itself wedge. This is a larger change (touches shared kthread-q semantics) and should be weighed against the simpler deferred-open COND fix that already breaks the cycle at its root (E1). Recommend the deferred-open COND fix as the primary; treat the bounded-flush as optional belt-and-suspenders, gated behind is_external_gpu.

openQuestions: rwsem ordering nuance: confirm the exact Linux rwsem fairness behavior for this kernel (7.0.9-204.fc44) — whether a queued WRITER (169991) blocks a NEW reader while a reader still holds (write-preferring). My reconstruction holds for the standard write-preferring rwsem; a vmcore read of g_RmApiLock.pLock->owner + wait_list would have nailed it, but no vmcore exists. The source-level cycle is sound regardless of fairness mode because 169991's WRITE acquire cannot proceed while ANY reader holds and 251420 cannot release until 169991 completes.

Should the F45 fix ALSO convert rmapiPrologue's CLIENT-module READ acquire (the actual starving holder) to conditional, or is breaking edge E1 (the deferred-open WRITE) sufficient? Breaking E1 alone lets 169991 complete -> unblocks 251420 and the flush, which should dissolve the whole cycle. Recommend E1-only as the minimal fix; converting rmapiPrologue is broader/riskier (it is a generic deep-RM path, not eGPU-scoped) and likely out of sovereignty scope.

Cold-bringup bounded-retry (RCA implication #3): a single stochastic H16 GSP transient strands the chip lost. Out of F45 deadlock scope, but worth a separate task — should the deferred/foreground open get a bounded GSP-init retry before declaring lost? This is reliability, not the deadlock fix.

Does making rm_get_adapter_status non-blocking interact with any NON-eGPU caller of rm_get_adapter_status_external that depends on a definitive (vs best-effort) adapter status? Grep shows the only consumer is nvlfp->adapter_status surfaced via NV_ESC_WAIT_OPEN_COMPLETE; confirm no internal RM caller treats NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM differently. If a guard is desired, gate the COND behavior behind nv->is_external_gpu to keep zero behavior change for dGPUs (mirrors A6/A7 gating).

Whether to fold F45 into the in-flight A10-v2 surgical work (task #293) or ship as a standalone A11 addon. They are conceptually adjacent (both COND_ACQUIRE-family, both racing gpu-lost teardown) but A10-v2 targets the C2/C5 sink fast-fail regression while F45 targets the deferred-open lock-inversion — distinct sites. Recommend standalone A11 for clean provenance; user to confirm bundling preference.

design.summary: F45 hardened fix design. The reconstructed cycle is a circular wait on the single global RM API rwsem (g_RmApiLock) where the unkillable nv_open_q deferred-open worker (pid 169991) is BLOCKED acquiring the API lock in WRITE mode inside rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2752 — a READ request silently stripped to WRITE by rmapi.c:572-579 because RM_LOCK_MODULES_OSAPI is not in the default CLIENT-only apiLockModuleMask, system.c:422). That blocked worker is the keystone: (E2) the SIGKILLed engage close (pid 251420) waits for the worker's complete_all (nv.c:1997) at nv_wait_open_complete (nv.c:2690/2661); (E4) the pciehp removal IRQ thread (pid 151) waits UNBOUNDED in nv_kthread_q_flush of nv_open_q (nv-pci.c:2451) behind the same in-flight worker; (E3) a stray nvidia-smi (pid 254369) is a second queued writer deepening starvation. Reboot-only because an unkillable kthread sits inside a kernel rwsem that FLR/TB-unauth/cable cannot move. Relaxed GSP locking is confirmed IRRELEVANT to F45: the failed cold-init thread cleanly releases the API lock (rm_init_adapter osapi.c:1777) and exits the cycle. MINIMAL FIX cuts edge E1 by making the deferred-open worker's rm_get_adapter_status acquire NON-BLOCKING (COND_ACQUIRE), gated to the eGPU. The worker can then never park on the API lock, always reaches complete_all, which dissolves E2/E3/E4 transitively. This is the exact C1 (osapi.c:1913) COND_ACQUIRE pattern applied to its uncovered sibling site, and is provably regression-free because adapter_status is best-effort telemetry whose contended value (NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM) is already the function's default return. Two optional defense-in-depth layers (deferred-open early-bail when device already gone; bounded open_q flush on the removal arm) are specified but NOT required for correctness.
findings: CUT EDGE = E1 (the keystone). The minimal fix makes the nv_open_q deferred-open worker's RM-API acquire NON-BLOCKING so pid-169991-class threads can NEVER park on g_RmApiLock. PROOF the cycle can no longer close: the cycle is E1->E2->E4 (and E3 as an amplifier). E2 (close 251420 waiting on open_complete) and E4 (pciehp 151 waiting on the open_q flush) BOTH depend exclusively on the deferred-open worker reaching complete_all (nv.c:1997) and returning from nvidia_open_deferred. The ONLY thing that prevented that was the blocking WRITE acquire inside rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2752) on the failure arm (nv.c:1787). If that acquire cannot block, the worker always runs to nv.c:1997, fires complete_all (unblocks E2), and returns so the open_q flush sentinel runs (unblocks E4). E3 (the stray nvidia-smi writer) was never load-bearing — it only deepened writer-starvation against a lock that, post-fix, the worker never contends for. With the keystone edge severed, no remaining set of the four actors forms a wait-for ring: there is no path from 251420/151/254369 back to a blocked 169991. Reboot is no longer required because the deadlock cannot form.

WHY THE eGPU GATE (pNv->is_external_gpu): mirrors A6/A7/C-series gating discipline (memory: A6/A7 gated on is_external_gpu). dGPUs never hit the F45 trigger geometry (no surprise-removal/pciehp flush racing a cold-init heartbeat fail) and there is no reason to perturb their adapter-status semantics. Gating keeps deep-RM blast radius to ZERO observable change for non-eGPU: the COND flag is only OR-ed in when is_external_gpu is true. If a reviewer prefers unconditional COND (argument: rm_get_adapter_status is best-effort for ALL GPUs and blocking it is never desirable), that is defensible and even simpler, but the gate is the conservative sovereignty-respecting default and matches the project's established A-series pattern. Recommend gated; flag the choice for reviewer sign-off.

RELAXED-GSP-LOCKING IS NOT A FIX SURFACE — confirmed from source, do not touch kgsp. _kgspBootGspRm releases the API lock across the heartbeat poll (kernel_gsp.c:4785) but _kgspBootReacquireLocks re-takes it (4818-4820) before returning; kgspInitRm asserts ownership after boot (5075) and its done: path releases ONLY the GPU group lock (5153-5156), never the API lock; the SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO 0xf failure (5105-5110) propagates with the API lock still held by the caller frame rm_init_adapter, which ALWAYS releases it on the failure return (osapi.c:1777). Therefore the cold-init thread is NOT the stranded holder and is NOT in the cycle. The relaxed-locking window only matters as the STOCHASTIC TRIGGER (it is when the H16 transient lands); it is not a lock-leak. No change to kernel_gsp.c is warranted or proposed.

C1 PRESERVED, NOT MODIFIED. C1 is the COND_ACQUIRE at rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state (osapi.c:1913, RM_LOCK_MODULES_DESTROY) covering the F44 sink acquire. F45's site is a DIFFERENT function (rm_get_adapter_status, RM_LOCK_MODULES_OSAPI). The A11 patch adds a second, independent COND_ACQUIRE at the sibling site and leaves osapi.c:1913 byte-for-byte intact. The two are non-overlapping hunks in the same file. Unified principle (stated for the patch-intent doc): every RM-API-lock acquire reachable from eGPU open/close/deferred/removal paths that can race a gpu-lost teardown must be COND_ACQUIRE-or-bail, never a blocking acquire that can park behind a held lock. F44 covered the sink (DESTROY); F45 covers the deferred-open status read (OSAPI). Same principle, distinct site -> distinct addon.

A6 BOUNDED-OPEN WORKER PRESERVED. A6's nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded (nv.c:1851) wraps only the FOREGROUND open (nv.c:2127). The F45 actor is the DEFERRED path (nvidia_open_deferred -> nv_open_device_for_nvlfp directly, nv.c:1989) which has no A6 bound. The A11 fix lives INSIDE rm_get_adapter_status (deep-RM), reachable from both paths, and changes only the lock-acquire mode — it does not touch the A6 wrapper, the worker dispatch, refcounting, or the foreground/deferred split. Orthogonal and non-conflicting. The R0 flush_work UAF guard (nv.c:1925-1957, on the A6 foreground timeout branch) is likewise untouched: A11 adds no new flush, no new free, and does not alter open_complete ordering — the worker still reaches the SAME complete_all at nv.c:1997, just without the possibility of blocking en route. No new UAF surface: COND_ACQUIRE only changes whether a lock is taken, never object lifetime.

DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH LAYER D1 (OPTIONAL, kernel-open, sovereignty-preferred) — early-bail the deferred-open status read when the device is already known-gone, so we don't even attempt the RM-API entry. SITE: nv_open_device_for_nvlfp, nv.c:1785-1788 (the else arm). Sketch: before calling rm_get_adapter_status_external, if the C5 sink already marks the GPU lost OR nv_check_gpu_state(nv)==NV_ERR_GPU_IS_LOST (pattern already at nv.c:1698-1703) OR os_pci_is_disconnected, set nvlfp->adapter_status = NV_ERR_GPU_IS_LOST and SKIP the external call entirely. This is strictly narrower than the primary fix (only the already-lost case) and is a kernel-open-only change, so it improves sovereignty posture and avoids the RM-API round-trip in the common F45 case (cold-init declared lost). It is a COMPLEMENT to, not a replacement for, the primary COND fix (the primary covers the transient-contention case where the device is not yet marked lost). Recommend shipping D1 alongside A11 only if the eGPU gate plumbing is already touching nv.c; otherwise the primary fix alone is sufficient.

DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH LAYER D2 (OPTIONAL, kernel-open, larger blast radius) — bound the removal-arm open_q flush so a future unknown stuck-worker class cannot wedge pciehp. SITE: the flush is nv_kthread_q_flush -> _raw_q_flush -> wait_for_completion with NO timeout (nv-kthread-q.c:301/304-317), invoked from nv_linux_stop_open_q in nv_pci_remove_helper (nv-pci.c:2451). A bounded variant (eGPU-gated, e.g. wait_for_completion_timeout + WARN on expiry) would let removal proceed even if a worker is wedged. This is belt-and-suspenders: with the primary fix the worker can no longer wedge on the API lock, so D2 is NOT required to break F45. It touches shared nv-kthread-q semantics (broader blast radius, risk of leaving a detached in-flight item) and should be weighed separately; recommend DEFERRING D2 unless a second independent worker-stall class is observed. Do not bundle D2 with A11.

RESIDUAL RISK (must be stated): (1) The primary fix makes adapter_status best-effort under contention — already true in practice (function defaults to NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM), and the value is consumed only as a diagnostic surfaced via NV_ESC_WAIT_OPEN_COMPLETE (nv.c:2990-2991), never as a control-flow gate for an internal RM caller (grep confirms rm_get_adapter_status_external's only consumer is nvlfp->adapter_status). Risk: LOW. (2) The fix breaks edge E1 but does NOT fix the underlying stochastic H16 cold-init failure — a single transient still strands the chip lost (chip is unusable until re-probe), it just no longer DEADLOCKS the host. That is the correct scope split: F45 is the deadlock; cold-bringup bounded-retry is a separate reliability task. (3) If, contrary to the reconstruction, the live holder were ever a WRITER that itself depends on the worker (not the reader-starvation model), breaking E1 still works: the worker no longer queues as a writer, so it cannot be in any writer-ordering ring. The fix is robust to the one unresolved open question (exact holder identity) precisely because it removes the worker from lock contention entirely rather than reasoning about who holds it.

LIVE VALIDATION (safe, NO kdump-on-wedged-eGPU). Primary proof is PREVENTION: the fix removes the only blocking acquire on the keystone path, so the wedge cannot form. To demonstrate positively without risking a host wedge: (A) drgn LIVE on the running patched host (read-only, no panic): after a forced cold-init failure, `drgn` script to walk the nv_open_q kthread task_struct and confirm it is NOT in rwsem_down_write_slowpath on g_RmApiLock, and that nvlfp->open_complete fires (completion done) — proves the worker drains. (B) fake-5090 lockdown-poll substrate (task #290): model the relaxed-GSP heartbeat-fail + concurrent close/removal traffic and assert no D-state pile-up on nv_open_q; this is the CI gate and the only place to safely exercise the trigger repeatedly. (C) On obpc, reproduce the F45 trigger geometry (cold modprobe + nvidia-smi -pm 1 under induced H16) with the patch and observe: dmesg shows RmInitAdapter 0x62:0xf failure (the trigger still fires) but NO 'task blocked for more than N seconds' hung-task on nv_open_q / irq/pciehp, and a subsequent re-probe succeeds without reboot. CRITICAL: do NOT arm kdump for this test (the capture kernel hangs re-probing the wedged eGPU per the 2026-06-02 RCA) — rely on passive dmesg + drgn live + the fact that a successful run simply does not wedge. If it DID wedge, capture via netconsole/serial, not kdump.
