Branch a11-f45-deferred-open-cond off the current addon-series tip in /root/open-gpu-kernel-modules; bump version.mk NVIDIA_VERSION to apnex.27; add A11 row to /root/nvidia-driver-injector/patches/manifest AFTER A10 (note A10 also still needs adding per the composition investigation); regen via tools/regen-base-patches.sh; author docs/patch-intents/A11-f45-deferred-open-cond.md from the schema/template cross-referencing F45 RCA + C1/A10 siblings; create the /lib/firmware/nvidia/595.71.05-apnex.27 symlink (task #294 gotcha or rm_init_adapter fails firmware load -2); VALIDATE by real `make modules` compile against the 7.0.9-204.fc44 kernel tree (project policy: apply-check is insufficient); add a fake-5090 F45 failure-mode doc sibling to F44.
openQuestions: GATE CHOICE: should the COND_ACQUIRE at rm_get_adapter_status be gated on pNv->is_external_gpu (conservative, zero dGPU behavior change, matches A6/A7 discipline — RECOMMENDED) or applied unconditionally (simpler, defensible since the function is best-effort for all GPUs)? Reviewers must sign off; verify pNv->is_external_gpu is reliably set on the deferred-open path at this point (A6 had a lazy-set coverage hole, task #287 — confirm is_external_gpu is set before the deferred worker reaches rm_get_adapter_status, or the gate silently no-ops and the fix never engages).

SINGLE-ATTEMPT vs RETRY: confirm reviewers agree the COND failure must be a single best-effort attempt returning NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM (NO retry loop). A retry/backoff would re-contend the lock the open_q flush needs to drain and could re-introduce a softer wedge. The design deliberately uses one attempt; stress-test that no caller treats a transient NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM adapter_status differently from a definitive one (grep NV_ESC_WAIT_OPEN_COMPLETE consumers + any internal rm_get_adapter_status_external caller).

3-ACTOR SUFFICIENCY: is the stray nvidia-smi (254369, E3) strictly required to seed the ring, or do E1+E2+E4 deadlock on their own? The design's cut (sever E1) works either way, but confirming E3 is non-essential validates that the fix needs no assumption about concurrent nvidia-smi load — important for the fake-5090 repro spec (task #290).

HOLDER IDENTITY (unresolved, fix-independent): the three investigations split on whether the live lock holder is the close-path READER (rmapiPrologue CLIENT-module read, not stripped) or a stray WRITER that won the relaxed-release window. The fix removes the worker from contention so it is robust to either — but reviewers should note that if it is the reader-starvation model, the close-path rmapiPrologue READ acquire is a generic deep-RM path (NOT eGPU-scoped) and converting it would be out of sovereignty scope; confirm severing E1 alone is accepted as sufficient without touching rmapiPrologue.

SYMBOL/OFFSET CROSS-CHECK: validate against the built apnex nvidia.ko that nvidia_close+0x56 is the wait_for_completion_interruptible at nv.c:2690 (state=S, no nvidia frames below strongly implies it) — a one-line objdump/addr2line on the deployed module confirms the 251420 edge and that the deferred-close re-wait (nv.c:2661, non-interruptible) is what makes SIGKILL ineffective, validating that Fix 1 (not a close-path change) is the right lever.

DEFERRED-PATH PRECONDITION: confirm pid 251420's engage fd genuinely took the DEFERRED open path (required for the 251420<->169991 edge). If it was a FOREGROUND open its open_complete already fired and the close wait is on a different fd — which would change the E2 edge but NOT the keystone-cut argument. Check the injector's nvidia-smi -pm 1 open pattern (O_NONBLOCK usage / fd count) to confirm the deferred path is the one in play.

critical: COND PRIMITIVE APPEARS INVERTED — this is the load-bearing blocker and the same 'false lock model' trap the brief warned about. The A11 fix's entire correctness rests on `API_LOCK_FLAGS_COND_ACQUIRE` behaving as: non-blocking, returns NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY on contention, acquires-and-holds on success. The actual unix primitive `os_cond_acquire_rwlock_write` (/root/open-gpu-kernel-modules/kernel-open/nvidia/os-interface.c:345-355) is: `if (down_write_trylock(&os_rwlock->sem)) return NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY; return NV_OK;`. The host kernel header (/usr/src/kernels/7.0.9-204.fc44.x86_64/include/linux/rwsem.h:245-247) documents `down_write_trylock` as 'returns 1 if successful, 0 if contention'. So when the trylock SUCCEEDS (lock acquired) the wrapper reports NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY (failure), and when it FAILS (contended) it reports NV_OK (success). This is inverted relative to NVIDIA's own correct usages: `os_cond_acquire_semaphore`/`mutex` use the SAME `if(trylock) return TIMEOUT_RETRY` pattern but `down_trylock` has the OPPOSITE convention (0=success), so those are correct; and nv-lock.h:50 uses `while(!down_read_trylock(lock))` which correctly treats nonzero as acquired. Consequence for A11 (and for the EXISTING A10/C1 at osapi.c:1913, which uses the identical primitive): on the contended F45 path the worker would believe it acquired the API lock (NV_OK) WITHOUT holding it, run the rm_get_adapter_status body, then call rmapiLockRelease()/up_write on a lock it never held -> rwsem count corruption / releasing another thread's lock -> a NEW and worse failure class than the deadlock being fixed. On the uncontended path it would return TIMEOUT_RETRY while having ACQUIRED+leaked the lock. MUST resolve empirically before any approval: build a tiny kernel test module (or drgn-inspect g_RmApiLock.pLock owner/count after a COND acquire on the live patched host) to determine the true runtime behavior of os_cond_acquire_rwlock_write. If it is inverted, A11 cannot ship on this primitive AND A10/C1 is already latently broken (project-wide finding); if it is somehow correct, document exactly why so the proof is not faith-based. The design's claim 'verified rmapi.c:605-612, write-conditional branch' inspected the CALLER but never the os-interface.c primitive it bottoms out in — that is the gap.

NO LOCK HOLDER IS EVER IDENTIFIED — the cycle is asserted, not proven. I confirmed from the captured stacks (wedge-...-stacks.txt, exactly 61 lines / 4 threads) that ALL FOUR named actors are WAITERS: 169991 (worker) and 254369 (smi) are blocked acquiring WRITE; 251420 (close) is in state S at nvidia_close+0x56 = nv_wait_open_complete (BEFORE nvidia_close_callback, so it holds NO API lock — verified nv.c:2690 and that rm_cleanup_file_private's lock acquire at osapi.c:3110 is downstream of the wait); 151 (pciehp) waits on the open_q flush. The cold-init GSP thread that failed RmInitAdapter cleanly RELEASES the API lock (verified: rm_init_adapter osapi.c:1761/1777 always rmapiLockRelease() on the failure return; kgspInitRm done: path at kernel_gsp.c:5132-5155 releases only the GPU group lock, and _kgspBootGspRm re-acquires the API lock via _kgspBootReacquireLocks at 4818-4820 before returning, asserted by rmapiLockIsOwner() at 5074). So NONE of the captured threads, and not the cold-init thread, holds the write lock that two writers are blocked on. A Linux rwsem WRITE acquire blocks only if someone holds it (or a prior writer is queued). Either there is an UNCAPTURED 5th holder (a running-on-CPU thread not dumped by sysrq-t) or this is transient writer-fairness starvation, NOT a permanent cycle — but the RCA says reboot was required after FLR/TB-unauth, implying a permanent hold. The design's defense ('the fix removes the worker from contention so it is robust to holder identity') is PARTIALLY valid (a trylock never enqueues, so post-fix the worker is never a blocked writer) BUT it does NOT save 254369 or 251420's later blocking cleanup acquire (osapi.c:3110, LOW_PRIORITY = blocking write) if the true holder is permanent. If a 5th thread permanently holds the lock, A11 unblocks ONLY 169991->complete_all->151, while 254369 and 251420-post-complete remain wedged forever -> host still needs reboot. The reconstruction must name the holder (re-examine the FULL sysrq-t for an R-state RM thread, or capture via netconsole next occurrence) before claiming the 4-actor cycle is the whole story.
concerns: RELAXED-GSP-LOCKING CLAIM IS CORRECT (verified, this one is NOT a false model) — independently confirmed against source, addressing the brief's explicit demand. _kgspBootGspRm releases the API lock at kernel_gsp.c:4785 only if _kgspShouldRelaxGspInitLocking (4689-4714, true by default), runs kgspBootstrap_HAL (where heartbeat times out), then ALWAYS re-acquires via _kgspBootReacquireLocks (4816-4820, blocking down_write at 4737) before returning; kgspInitRm asserts rmapiLockIsOwner() at 5074, and the SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO 0xf failure (5105-5108) goto done releases only the GPU group lock (5153-5155). The API lock unwinds cleanly through rm_init_adapter's rmapiLockRelease (osapi.c:1777). So relaxed locking is the stochastic TRIGGER window, not a leak. No kgsp change warranted — design's conclusion stands. One nuance the design omits: _kgspBootReacquireLocks's re-acquire is itself a BLOCKING down_write wrapped in NV_ASSERT_OK_OR_RETURN; if it ever failed to re-acquire it would return with locks in an indeterminate state, but on Linux it 'should never fail' per the comment — acceptable.

READ-STRIP MECHANISM CONFIRMED — the claim that rm_get_adapter_status's RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_READ is silently promoted to WRITE is correct: apiLockModuleMask defaults to RM_LOCK_MODULE_GRP(RM_LOCK_MODULES_CLIENT) only (system.c:422), RM_LOCK_MODULES_OSAPI is not in it, so rmapi.c:570-579 strips the READ bit; the 169991 stack ending in os_acquire_rwlock_write confirms the runtime promotion. osApiLockAcquireConfigureFlags is a no-op on unix (os.c:3140, returns flags unchanged), so an OR-ed-in COND flag survives. The flag ordering in the proposed sketch is fine: READ gets stripped first (line 572), then the COND branch (605) takes the write-conditional path. This part of the analysis is sound.

GATE VALIDITY CONFIRMED (resolves the design's own openQuestion #1) — pNv->is_external_gpu is set at PROBE time in nv_pci_probe (nv-pci.c:2042 via os_pci_is_thunderbolt_attached), and the A6 lazy-set hole (task #287) was fixed by commit 6a25b26c (A9 probe-time classification). So the eGPU gate WILL be set before the deferred worker reaches rm_get_adapter_status; the gate will not silently no-op. The gated-vs-unconditional choice is a reasonable reviewer decision; gated matches A6/A7 discipline.

NO-RETRY / NO-LIVELOCK DISCIPLINE IS CORRECT — assuming the primitive worked, the single-attempt design is right: rm_get_adapter_status_external folds any non-NV_OK to NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM (osapi.c:2773-2776), nv_open_device_for_nvlfp returns the already-non-zero open_rc unchanged (nv.c:1779-1789), no caller spins. The deferred worker reaches complete_all (nv.c:1997) and returns to _main_loop, which then runs the flush sentinel. This correctly avoids the busy-wait-against-the-lock-the-flush-needs livelock. (Contingent on Critical #1 being resolved favorably.)

E4 FLUSH-EDGE MECHANICS VERIFIED — nv_pci_remove_helper at nv-pci.c:2451 calls nv_linux_stop_open_q -> nv_kthread_q_stop -> nv_kthread_q_flush (nv-kthread-q.c:304), which schedules a sentinel and wait_for_completion with NO timeout (_raw_q_flush, line 301-304); _main_loop (nv-kthread-q.c:58-78) runs queued items inline and single-threaded, so the sentinel cannot run until the in-flight nvidia_open_deferred returns. Confirmed unbounded behind the worker. The design's D2 (bounded flush) is correctly characterized as optional/defer.

A6/UAF/C1 NON-INTERFERENCE CONFIRMED — the deferred path (nvidia_open_deferred, nv.c:1989) calls nv_open_device_for_nvlfp DIRECTLY, bypassing the A6 bounded wrapper (nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded only wraps the foreground open at nv.c:2127, and self-gates on is_external_gpu/timeout). A11 lives inside rm_get_adapter_status (deep-RM), touching only lock-acquire mode, so it does not alter the A6 wrapper, the R0 flush_work UAF guard (nv.c:1925-1957), or the complete_all ordering. C1 at osapi.c:1913 is a distinct function (rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state, RM_LOCK_MODULES_DESTROY) and would be left byte-for-byte intact. These orthogonality claims hold — EXCEPT that C1 shares the suspect primitive (see Critical #1), so 'C1 is validated' cannot be used as evidence the primitive works.
suggestedChanges: BLOCKER-RESOLUTION FIRST: empirically determine the true runtime behavior of os_cond_acquire_rwlock_write / portSyncRwLockAcquireWriteConditional before writing any A11 code. Minimal safe test: a standalone kernel module that init_rwsem()s a lock, calls down_write_trylock twice (second should fail), and logs the return values; OR drgn on the live patched host to read g_RmApiLock.pLock->rwlock owner/count immediately after an instrumented COND acquire. If the primitive is inverted as the source strongly indicates, file it as a project-wide CRITICAL (A10/C1 is also affected) and DO NOT build A11 on API_LOCK_FLAGS_COND_ACQUIRE until the primitive is fixed or a correct conditional-acquire path is identified.

If the primitive IS inverted: the cleanest fix is to correct os_cond_acquire_rwlock_write and os_cond_acquire_rwlock_read in kernel-open/nvidia/os-interface.c (invert the condition to `if (!down_write_trylock(...)) return NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY; return NV_OK;`), which simultaneously fixes A10/C1 and unblocks A11 — but this is a deep, high-blast-radius change to a primitive used by ALL conditional API-lock acquires, so it needs its own intent doc, a from-scratch audit of every COND_ACQUIRE caller, and a real `make modules` compile + targeted runtime test. Alternatively, if NVIDIA's intent was genuinely this convention (extremely unlikely given the cross-references), document the proof exhaustively.

Identify the actual lock holder before finalizing the cycle: on the next F45 occurrence (or via the fake-5090 substrate, task #290), capture the FULL sysrq-t (not just D-state threads) over netconsole/serial — NOT kdump (the capture kernel hangs re-probing the wedged eGPU per the 2026-06-02 RCA) — and look for an R-state RM thread holding g_RmApiLock, or use drgn to read g_RmApiLock.threadId and map it to a PID. Without a named holder the '4-actor circular wait' is a hypothesis, and the fix's sufficiency for 254369/251420 is unproven.

Strengthen residual-risk (3): the design says the fix is robust if the holder is a writer 'because the worker no longer queues as a writer.' That only proves 169991 escapes; it does NOT prove 254369 (a blocking writer at serverTopLock_Prologue) or 251420's post-complete rm_cleanup_file_private (blocking LOW_PRIORITY write at osapi.c:3110) drain. State explicitly: A11 guarantees only that 169991 reaches complete_all and the open_q flush (151) drains; full host recovery additionally REQUIRES that the original holder was transient (all source evidence supports this — no permanent-holder path found — but it is an assumption, not a proof, until the holder is named).

Prefer the D1 kernel-open early-bail (nv.c:1785-1788) as the PRIMARY mechanism rather than optional, IF the COND primitive turns out unreliable: gating the deferred-open status read on nv_check_gpu_state(nv)==NV_ERR_GPU_IS_LOST (the existing pattern at nv.c:1698-1703 / 2743-2744) OR os_pci_is_disconnected avoids the RM-API round-trip entirely on the common F45 'already declared lost' case, breaking E1 WITHOUT depending on the suspect conditional-acquire primitive at all. This is the more sovereignty-respecting and primitive-independent cut. The COND_ACQUIRE then only needs to cover the rarer transient-contention-but-not-yet-lost case.

Add a positive assertion test to the validation plan: after a COND acquire reports NV_OK, assert rmapiLockIsOwner()==true (the RM already has this helper, used at kernel_gsp.c:5074); after it reports TIMEOUT_RETRY, assert rmapiLockIsOwner()==false. This single assertion pair would have caught the inversion immediately and should gate the A11 (and a retro-audit of A10/C1) before any soak.

Bump version to apnex.27 and create the matching /lib/firmware/nvidia/595.71.05-apnex.27 symlink BEFORE first modprobe (task #294 gotcha — the 2026-06-02 wedge itself started because apnex.26 loaded before its firmware symlink existed). Validate with a real `make modules` against 7.0.9-204.fc44 per project policy (apply-check is insufficient — the P5 nv-misc.h precedent).

concerns: INCOMPLETE CONSUMER ENUMERATION (the one substantive miss). The PRIMARY fix modifies rm_get_adapter_status (the INNER function, osapi.c:2744), NOT just the _external wrapper. The design's residual-risk finding (1) asserts 'rm_get_adapter_status_external's only consumer is nvlfp->adapter_status' and 'the value is consumed only as a diagnostic surfaced via NV_ESC_WAIT_OPEN_COMPLETE.' That is FALSE for the function being patched: there is a SECOND, direct caller of rm_get_adapter_status at /root/open-gpu-kernel-modules/src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/src/escape.c:779 — the NV_ESC_STATUS_CODE ioctl, which queries adapter status by domain/bus/slot and does `if (rmStatus != NV_OK) goto done` (escape.c:781-782), returning the status to userspace. Under the gated fix, on an is_external_gpu device this userspace ioctl would, under lock contention, return NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM (best-effort) instead of blocking until it can read the true status. The impact is BENIGN (a status-query ioctl, not an internal control-flow gate; the eGPU gate scopes it; no stranded state) and does NOT undermine the keystone-cut argument — but the design's claim that adapter_status has a single consumer is incorrect and the escape.c path must be enumerated in the patch-intent doc. This is a documentation/completeness defect, not a correctness defect in the fix.

GATE NO-OP RISK IS ALREADY RESOLVED (design under-credits its own dependency). Open question #GATE-CHOICE frames it as unverified whether pNv->is_external_gpu is reliably set before the deferred worker reaches rm_get_adapter_status ('or the gate silently no-ops and the fix never engages'). Verified: A9 sets nv->is_external_gpu at PROBE time via os_pci_is_thunderbolt_attached(nv->handle) at /root/open-gpu-kernel-modules/kernel-open/nvidia/nv-pci.c:2042, explicitly to close the lazy-set hole (task #287), and the comment states it is monotonic (nothing writes it FALSE). Since probe precedes any open, the deferred-open worker WILL see the flag set. The gate engages. The design should state A9 as the enabling precondition rather than leaving it open — and note that the fix's correctness now DEPENDS on A9 being composed ahead of A11 in the manifest (an ordering constraint worth recording).

MINOR LINE-NUMBER DRIFT in kernel_gsp.c fileRefs (substance correct, offsets stale). Design cites kernel_gsp.c:5075 and 5153 for the kgspInitRm assert/done-path; actual: _kgspBootGspRm call is at 5064, SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO failure at 5105-5109 (design said 5105-5110), done: label at 5129, and the GPU-group-lock-only release at 5153-5156 (design's 5153 is the start of that block, acceptable). The load-bearing claim — that the kgspInitRm done: path releases ONLY the GPU group lock and the API lock is released by the caller rm_init_adapter at osapi.c:1777 on the failure return — is VERIFIED TRUE. The drift is cosmetic; fix the offsets when authoring the patch-intent so they don't rot further.
suggestedChanges: Enumerate the SECOND caller in the patch-intent + residual-risk: rm_get_adapter_status is called directly from the NV_ESC_STATUS_CODE ioctl at src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/src/escape.c:779 (in addition to the deferred-open _external wrapper at nv.c:1787). State explicitly that, on a contended eGPU, this userspace status-query ioctl now returns NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM best-effort instead of blocking, and confirm that is acceptable (it is — userspace already must treat a status query as fallible). This also strengthens the case for the eGPU GATE over unconditional COND, since unconditional COND would change escape.c:779 behavior for ALL GPUs.

Record the A9 -> A11 composition ORDERING constraint: the primary fix's eGPU gate (pNv->is_external_gpu) only engages because A9 (nv-pci.c:2042) sets that flag at probe. A11 MUST compose after A9 in /root/nvidia-driver-injector/patches/manifest, and a build without A9 would silently disable the F45 fix (gate reads FALSE). Add this to the integration finding and the intent-doc dependencies section. Close open question #GATE-CHOICE's no-op sub-concern with the A9 evidence rather than leaving it open.

Correct the kernel_gsp.c fileRefs offsets in the design: SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO failure is at 5105-5109, done: at 5129, GPU-group-lock release at 5153-5156, _kgspBootGspRm call at 5064. Keep 4785 (rmapiLockRelease) and 4818 (_kgspBootReacquireLocks) — both verified exact.
Add escape.c:779 to fileRefs (the missed second caller) so the patch-intent's site map is complete.

Optional, strengthens the 'single-attempt not retry' decision (open question #2): note that escape.c:779 is the one path where a caller propagates the rmStatus to userspace as an error code; a retry loop there would be observable as latency. The single best-effort attempt is correct for BOTH the telemetry consumer (nv.c:2991) and the ioctl consumer (escape.c:779).

concerns: E4 FILE:LINE IMPRECISION (non-blocking, fix the ref). The design's keystone E4 edge cites the removal-arm flush as 'nv_kthread_q_flush ... nv_pci_remove_helper (nv-pci.c:2451)'. Verified: nv-pci.c:2451 actually calls nv_linux_stop_open_q(nvl), which (nv.c:6200) calls nv_kthread_q_stop -> nv_kthread_q_flush (nv-kthread-q.c:113) -> _raw_q_flush -> unbounded wait_for_completion (nv-kthread-q.c:289/301). The UNBOUNDED wait behind the in-flight worker is REAL and the E4 argument holds, but the cited symbol at 2451 is the stop wrapper, not a bare flush. (A direct nv_kthread_q_flush(&nvl->open_q) does exist, but on the PM-suspend path at nv.c:4898, not the removal path.) Correct the fileRef/finding to name nv_linux_stop_open_q -> nv_kthread_q_stop so the proof chain is exact and the optional D2 layer targets the right function.

is_external_gpu GATE TIMING IS ACTUALLY SOLID FOR THE DEFERRED PATH — the design under-sells it as an open question. Verified pNv->is_external_gpu (nv.h:556) is set at PROBE time in nv_pci_probe (nv-pci.c:2042, os_pci_is_thunderbolt_attached), which runs long before any open. The task #287 'lazy-set coverage hole' was a FOREGROUND-A6 concern; on the deferred path the field is reliably true before nvidia_open_deferred -> rm_get_adapter_status_external. So the gate will NOT silently no-op for F45. Recommend converting openQuestion #1's worry into an affirmative claim (gate engages) rather than leaving it as a risk; still worth a one-line runtime assert/print in the patch for belt-and-suspenders.

DEEP-RM vs KERNEL-OPEN BLAST RADIUS — the primary fix is genuinely deep-RM but the line count is minimal and justified. The blocking acquire is physically inside rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c, a deep-RM TU); there is no kernel-open seam wrapping it (rm_get_adapter_status_external is just a thin NV_ENTER/EXIT_RM_RUNTIME shim, osapi.c:2763, with no lock logic to intercept). The proposed change is ~3 lines (flag var + gate + OR), identical in placement and pattern to C1/A10 which ALREADY edit this same file. The lower-blast-radius kernel-open alternative (D1 early-bail at nv.c:1785) does NOT replace the primary: D1 only covers the already-marked-lost case; the primary covers the transient-contention case where the device is not yet marked lost. Design states this correctly. Concern is only that the openQuestion #4 framing ('if reader-starvation model, converting close-path rmapiPrologue would be out of scope') correctly declines to expand blast radius — endorse that restraint.

RESIDUAL: the fix does NOT address the stochastic H16 cold-init failure itself (chip still stranded-lost until re-probe); it only removes the DEADLOCK. The design states this as residual risk #2 and correctly scopes cold-bringup bounded-retry as separate. No objection, but ensure the F45 catalog entry and patch-intent are explicit that A11 is a deadlock-breaker, NOT a bringup-reliability fix, so a future reader does not assume cold-init is now robust.
suggestedChanges: UAF / R0 GUARD: CONFIRMED PRESERVED, no new UAF. The R0 flush_work UAF guard lives in the FOREGROUND nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded (guard+flush at nv.c:1925-1957); the F45 actor is the DEFERRED path (nvidia_open_deferred -> nv_open_device_for_nvlfp directly at nv.c:1989) which has neither the A6 wrapper nor the R0 guard. A11 changes only the lock-acquire MODE inside rm_get_adapter_status (deep-RM, reachable from both paths) — it adds no flush, no free, no completion-ordering change. The deferred worker still reaches the SAME complete_all at nv.c:1997. COND_ACQUIRE alters whether a lock is taken, never object lifetime, so it introduces ZERO new UAF surface. Deferred-worker nvlfp lifetime remains protected by is_accepting_opens + open_q stop/flush (edge E4), which A11 does not touch. No change needed; just keep this reasoning in the patch-intent.

NORMAL (HEALTHY) PATH IS UNTOUCHED — STRENGTHEN THE CLAIM. Verified rm_get_adapter_status_external is called ONLY on the open FAILURE arm: nv_open_device_for_nvlfp sets adapter_status=NV_OK and SKIPS the external call when open_rc==0 (nv.c:1781-1787). So a healthy eGPU cold-bringup never reaches the modified function at all; a healthy dGPU is excluded by the is_external_gpu gate anyway. The common case is doubly-untouched (gate AND failure-arm-only). Add this to the regression argument — it is stronger than the design currently states (the design leans on 'adapter_status already defaults to NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM' but the cleaner point is the modified code is unreachable on success).

tb_egpu_state STRANDING: NOT A RISK for A11. Verified A11's site (rm_get_adapter_status) does NOT call nv_tb_egpu_f40b_fired() or any A8 state transition (those live in the A6/A7 timeout branches at nv.c:1923 / 2342). A11 only reads adapter status; it cannot strand tb_egpu_state. The C5 sink / lost-temporary transition is driven by the ALREADY-FAILED rm_init_adapter path, independent of A11. Note this explicitly in the intent so a reviewer does not conflate A11 with the A8 state machine.

A10-v2 COMPOSITION: CONFIRMED CONFLICT-FREE. A10-v2 (task #293) edits kernel_gsp_gh100.c:1050 and adds field tb_egpu_in_lockdown_poll to nv_state_t (nv.h ~556 region). A11 edits osapi.c:rm_get_adapter_status and reuses the EXISTING is_external_gpu field. Zero file/hunk overlap; the only shared touch is additive fields in the same header region (mergeable, no layout conflict). Recommend landing order: A10/A10-v2 first (they fix the F44 sink/poll), then A11 on top — the manifest already needs A10 added per the composition investigation. Keep A11 STANDALONE (a11-f45-deferred-open-cond) as the design proposes; do NOT fold into task #293.

CATALOG ATTRIBUTION: CORRECT. Cross-checked against the RCA (wedge-2026-06-02-coldboot-apilock-deadlock.md): trigger = cold nvidia-smi -pm 1 first-open -> stochastic H16 PCIe transient -> GSP heartbeat timeout / SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO 0xf -> RmInitAdapter 0x62:0xf:2131 -> C5 sink (verified). Cycle = 4 actors (deferred nv_open_q worker blocked on WRITE acquire; SIGKILLed engage-close in non-interruptible nv_wait_open_complete; pciehp removal flushing open_q; stray nvidia-smi writer) — verified against the stacks. Sibling-of-F44 = distinct trigger (cold-init heartbeat fail vs A6/A7 timeout) and distinct site (rm_get_adapter_status OSAPI vs rm_cleanup DESTROY), same global rwsem class — verified. Fix = COND_ACQUIRE on the C1-UNCOVERED sibling site — verified. Also independently verified: rm_init_adapter takes API_LOCK_FLAGS_NONE (blocking) at osapi.c:1761 and ALWAYS releases at osapi.c:1777 on failure, so the cold-init thread is NOT the stranded holder and relaxed-GSP-locking is correctly ruled OUT as a fix surface.

SYMBOL CHECK: the sketch's API_LOCK_FLAGS_COND_ACQUIRE is valid — it is an alias defined at core/locks.h:193 -> rmapi.h:64 (RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_COND_ACQUIRE = NVBIT(0)), and is exactly the spelling C1 already uses at osapi.c:1913. The READ flag is RMAPI_LOCK_FLAGS_READ (rmapi.h:66). No symbol issue; keep the C1-consistent spelling.

COND-FAILURE-PATH CORRECTNESS: CONFIRMED. Verified rmapi.c:570-579 strips READ->WRITE for RM_LOCK_MODULES_OSAPI (default apiLockModuleMask = CLIENT-only, system.c:422), and the COND write branch (rmapi.c:603-612) returns NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY WITHOUT blocking when the write-conditional fails. rm_get_adapter_status then returns its pre-initialized NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM, which rm_get_adapter_status_external folds to NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM (osapi.c:2773-2776) — bit-identical to the contended/lost case. SINGLE attempt, NO retry loop, is correct (a retry would re-contend the lock the open_q flush needs to drain). adapter_status is consumed only as a userspace diagnostic at NV_ESC_WAIT_OPEN_COMPLETE (nv.c:2991), never as an internal control-flow gate — verified by grep. Zero stranded state; worker proceeds to complete_all (nv.c:1997). Endorse as-is.

final.summary:
F45 — cold-bringup RM-API rwsem deadlock (obpc, 2026-06-02)

TRIGGER: A COLD first-open of the TB4 eGPU (RTX 5090 / GB202) via `nvidia-smi -pm 1` after fresh modprobe hits a stochastic H16-class PCIe transient on the TB bridge during GSP boot ("pcieport 0000:03:00.0: AER: device recovery failed" + GSP heartbeat timeout "_kgspRpcRecvPoll: GSP RM heartbeat timed out" / "SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO failed 0xf") -> "RmInitAdapter failed (0x62:0xf:2131)" -> the C5 sink declares the GPU lost. The cold-init thread itself releases the API lock cleanly (rm_init_adapter osapi.c:1777 always rmapiLockRelease on the failure return) so it is NOT the stranded holder; relaxed GSP init locking is only the stochastic window, not a lock leak.

CYCLE (circular wait on the single global RM API rwsem g_RmApiLock; reconstructed from 4 sysrq-t stacks — NO vmcore): the unkillable single-threaded nv_open_q deferred-open worker (pid 169991) is blocked acquiring the API lock in WRITE mode inside rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2752 — a READ request silently stripped to WRITE for RM_LOCK_MODULES_OSAPI by rmapi.c:572-578, because OSAPI is not in the default CLIENT-only apiLockModuleMask, system.c:422). That blocked worker is the keystone: (E2) the SIGKILLed engage close (pid 251420, nvidia_close+0x56 = nv_wait_open_complete, nv.c:2690/2661) waits for the worker's complete_all (nv.c:1997); (E4) the pciehp removal IRQ thread (pid 151) waits UNBOUNDED in nv_kthread_q_flush of nv_open_q (via nv_linux_stop_open_q -> nv_kthread_q_stop, nv-pci.c:2451) behind the same in-flight worker; (E3) a stray nvidia-smi (pid 254369) is a second queued API-lock writer amplifying starvation. No captured thread and not the cold-init thread holds the write lock the two writers block on — the true permanent holder is UNNAMED (no vmcore; likely a 5th uncaptured R-state RM thread or transient writer-fairness starvation made permanent by the unkillable kthread sitting in the rwsem).

IMMUNE TO: SIGKILL, FLR (PCI function reset), TB unauthorize+reauthorize, cable pull. Reboot-only, because an unkillable kthread sits inside a kernel rwsem that none of those host-side resets can move.

DECISIVE NEW FINDING (overrides the proposed A11): the COND_ACQUIRE primitive the entire fix relies on is INVERTED for the API rwsem. os_cond_acquire_rwlock_write (kernel-open/nvidia/os-interface.c:345-355) does `if (down_write_trylock(sem)) return NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY; return NV_OK;` but down_write_trylock returns 1=acquired / 0=contended (kernel rwsem.h:245-247 "returns 1 if successful, 0 if contention"), the OPPOSITE convention to down_trylock (0=success) which the semaphore/mutex variants correctly use. Tracing through portSyncRwLockAcquireWriteConditional (sync_unix_kernel_os.c:295) and rmapi.c:603-612: on SUCCESSFUL acquire the chain reports NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY (and leaks the held lock), on CONTENTION it reports NV_OK (and runs the body + rmapiLockRelease on a lock it never held -> rwsem count corruption). The ONLY two API-lock COND_ACQUIRE consumers in the tree are rmapi.c:595 (impl) and osapi.c:1913 (the shipped C1/A10). Therefore the proposed A11 (a 2nd COND_ACQUIRE at osapi.c:2752) would be built on a broken primitive AND C1/A10 is already latently broken in production. GPU-lock COND callers (locks.c, portSyncSpinlock-based) are a different path and are NOT affected.

FIX (revised, two parts): (1) PRIMITIVE FIX — correct os_cond_acquire_rwlock_write and os_cond_acquire_rwlock_read in os-interface.c to `if (!down_{write,read}_trylock(sem)) return NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY; return NV_OK;`. This simultaneously repairs the shipped C1/A10 and makes any rwsem COND_ACQUIRE actually non-blocking. (2) F45 SITE FIX — only AFTER (1), apply the eGPU-gated COND_ACQUIRE at rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2752) to cut the keystone edge E1 so the nv_open_q worker can never park on g_RmApiLock and always reaches complete_all (dissolving E2/E3/E4 transitively). A primitive-independent alternative/complement (kernel-open early-bail D1 at nv.c:1785) breaks E1 WITHOUT depending on the conditional-acquire primitive when the device is already marked lost.

SIBLING-OF: F44 (the A6/A7-timeout rm_cleanup lock-inversion). Same global-rwsem class, DISTINCT trigger (cold-init heartbeat fail vs A6/A7 timeout) and DISTINCT site (rm_get_adapter_status OSAPI deferred-open status read vs rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state DESTROY sink).
findings: KEYSTONE CUT = edge E1 (the deferred-open worker's blocking WRITE acquire). The minimal cut makes the nv_open_q worker's RM-API acquire at rm_get_adapter_status NON-BLOCKING so a pid-169991-class thread can NEVER park on g_RmApiLock. Proof the cycle cannot close: E2 (close 251420 in nv_wait_open_complete) and E4 (pciehp 151 in the open_q flush) BOTH depend exclusively on the worker reaching complete_all (nv.c:1997) and returning from nvidia_open_deferred. The only thing that prevented that was the blocking WRITE acquire inside rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2752) on the open-FAILURE arm (nv.c:1787). If that acquire cannot block, the worker always runs to nv.c:1997, fires complete_all (unblocks E2), and returns so the open_q flush sentinel runs (unblocks E4). E3 (the stray nvidia-smi writer) was never load-bearing. CAVEAT (reviewer #1, accepted): cutting E1 guarantees 169991 escapes and 151 drains; FULL host recovery additionally requires that the unnamed permanent holder was transient. All source evidence supports transience (no permanent-holder leak path was found — cold-init releases cleanly at osapi.c:1777), but with no vmcore this is an ASSUMPTION, not a proof. If a 5th thread permanently holds the write lock, A11 unblocks 169991->151 but leaves 254369 and 251420's post-complete cleanup acquire (rm_cleanup_file_private blocking write, osapi.c:3110) wedged -> reboot still needed. State this explicitly in the F45 catalog and intent doc.

BLOCKER RESOLVED FROM SOURCE — THE COND_ACQUIRE PRIMITIVE IS INVERTED FOR THE API RWSEM (reviewer #1 critical CONFIRMED). os_cond_acquire_rwlock_write (kernel-open/nvidia/os-interface.c:345-355): `if (down_write_trylock(&os_rwlock->sem)) return NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY; return NV_OK;`. The host kernel header documents down_write_trylock as 'returns 1 if successful, 0 if contention' (/usr/src/kernels/7.0.9-204.fc44.x86_64/include/linux/rwsem.h:245-247). So acquired(1)->NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY and contended(0)->NV_OK — backwards. Full chain: portSyncRwLockAcquireWriteConditional (sync_unix_kernel_os.c:295) returns `(... == NV_OK)`, so acquired->NvFalse / contended->NvTrue; rmapi.c:605 `if(portSync...) {threadId=...} else {rmStatus=NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY}` therefore: ACQUIRED -> else branch -> reports TIMEOUT_RETRY while HOLDING+LEAKING the lock; CONTENDED -> records threadId + returns NV_OK -> caller runs the protected body and calls rmapiLockRelease()/up_write on a lock it never held -> rwsem count corruption / releasing another thread's lock. The semaphore/mutex variants (os_cond_acquire_mutex/semaphore, os-interface.c:151/228) are CORRECT because down_trylock has the opposite Linux convention (0=success); the rwlock variants were written with the down_trylock body but the rwsem trylock convention is reversed. ROOT FIX REQUIRED.

PART 1 (PRIMITIVE FIX, REQUIRED, ships first) — SITE: /root/open-gpu-kernel-modules/kernel-open/nvidia/os-interface.c:333-355. Invert both conditionals:
  os_cond_acquire_rwlock_read:  change `if (down_read_trylock(&os_rwlock->sem)) return NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY; return NV_OK;`  ->  `if (!down_read_trylock(&os_rwlock->sem)) return NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY; return NV_OK;`
  os_cond_acquire_rwlock_write: change `if (down_write_trylock(&os_rwlock->sem)) return NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY; return NV_OK;`  ->  `if (!down_write_trylock(&os_rwlock->sem)) return NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY; return NV_OK;`
BLAST RADIUS is bounded and SAFE: the ONLY consumers of these two rwsem-cond primitives are the API-lock COND_ACQUIRE path (rmapi.c:595->portSyncRwLockAcquire{Read,Write}Conditional), and the ONLY API-lock COND_ACQUIRE call site in the whole tree is osapi.c:1913 (the shipped C1/A10). GPU-lock COND_ACQUIRE callers (unix_intr.c, ccsl.c, kernel_rc_callback.c, os_timer.c, control.c, nv_gpu_ops.c, lock_stress.c) go through locks.c on a portSyncSpinlock, NOT this rwsem wrapper, and are UNAFFECTED. Consequence: this fix simultaneously REPAIRS the latently-broken C1/A10 (today, on the F44 contended path, C1 either leaks the lock on success or releases an unheld lock on contention — the F44 fix is currently a corruption hazard, not a working mitigation) and makes Part 2 possible. This is a deep kernel-open change to a shared primitive but it is OUR file (kernel-open/), 2 one-character edits, with a fully enumerated 1-site live consumer; it needs its own patch-intent and a real compile, but the audit is complete here.

TWO CALLERS OF THE PATCHED INNER FUNCTION (reviewer #2 finding CONFIRMED — corrects the design's single-consumer claim). rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2744) is called from TWO sites, not one: (a) rm_get_adapter_status_external (osapi.c:2773), the deferred-open failure arm, whose result lands only in nvlfp->adapter_status and is surfaced solely as a userspace diagnostic via NV_ESC_WAIT_OPEN_COMPLETE (nv.c:2990-2991) — never an internal control-flow gate; and (b) the NV_ESC_STATUS_CODE ioctl at /root/open-gpu-kernel-modules/src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/src/escape.c:779, which does `if (rmStatus != NV_OK) goto done` (escape.c:781) and returns the status to userspace. Under the eGPU gate, on a contended eGPU this status-query ioctl returns NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM best-effort instead of blocking — BENIGN (a status query is inherently fallible; the gate scopes it to eGPU; no stranded state) but it MUST be enumerated in the intent doc, and it strengthens the case for the eGPU GATE over unconditional COND (unconditional would change escape.c:779 for all GPUs). Add escape.c:779 to the site map.

NORMAL (HEALTHY) PATH IS DOUBLY UNTOUCHED (reviewer #3, accepted — stronger than the design stated). rm_get_adapter_status_external is reached ONLY on the open-FAILURE arm: nv_open_device_for_nvlfp sets adapter_status=NV_OK and SKIPS the external call when open_rc==0 (nv.c:1781-1787). So a healthy eGPU cold-bringup never reaches the modified function at all, and a healthy dGPU is excluded by the is_external_gpu gate. The contended-COND behavior change is observable only on (eGPU AND open-already-failed AND lock-contended) — the exact F45 geometry. This is a cleaner regression argument than 'the function already defaults to NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM'.

RELAXED GSP LOCKING IS NOT A FIX SURFACE — confirmed, no kgsp change. _kgspBootGspRm releases the API lock across the heartbeat poll (kernel_gsp.c:4785, only when _kgspShouldRelaxGspInitLocking is true — default on this consumer 5090) but _kgspBootReacquireLocks blocking-re-takes it (4818-4820) before returning; kgspInitRm asserts rmapiLockIsOwner after boot (~5064) and its done: path releases ONLY the GPU group lock (5153-5156), never the API lock; the SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO 0xf failure (5105-5109) propagates with the API lock still held by the caller frame rm_init_adapter, which ALWAYS rmapiLockRelease on the failure return (osapi.c:1777). So the cold-init thread is NOT the stranded holder and is NOT in the cycle. Relaxed locking is only the STOCHASTIC TRIGGER window (when the H16 transient lands), not a lock leak. NOTE: kernel_gsp.c fileRef offsets in the original design drifted (SET_GUEST_SYSTEM_INFO fail is 5105-5109 not 5105-5110; done: ~5129; _kgspBootGspRm call ~5064; group-lock release 5153-5156) — load-bearing claim verified true, fix the offsets when authoring the intent.

C1/A10 RELATIONSHIP REFRAMED. C1/A10 is the COND_ACQUIRE at rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state (osapi.c:1913, RM_LOCK_MODULES_DESTROY). It is NOT 'preserved byte-for-byte' as the original design assumed — it is the OTHER (currently broken) consumer of the inverted primitive and is FIXED-IN-PLACE by Part 1 (no edit to osapi.c:1913 itself; the primitive underneath it is corrected). A11 then ADDS a second, independent, now-correct COND_ACQUIRE at the sibling site (osapi.c:2752, RM_LOCK_MODULES_OSAPI). Unified principle for the intent doc: every RM-API-lock acquire reachable from eGPU open/close/deferred/removal paths that can race a gpu-lost teardown must be COND_ACQUIRE-or-bail over a CORRECT conditional primitive, never a blocking acquire that can park behind a held lock. F44 covered the sink (DESTROY); F45 covers the deferred-open status read (OSAPI); both depend on the Part-1 primitive being correct.

A6 / UAF / tb_egpu_state NON-INTERFERENCE CONFIRMED (reviewer #3). The F45 actor is the DEFERRED path (nvidia_open_deferred -> nv_open_device_for_nvlfp directly, nv.c:1989), which bypasses the A6 bounded wrapper (nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded wraps only the FOREGROUND open at nv.c:2127). A11/A11 live inside rm_get_adapter_status / the rwsem primitive, changing only lock-acquire MODE — not the A6 wrapper, the worker dispatch, refcounting, the foreground/deferred split, or the R0 flush_work UAF guard (nv.c:1925-1957). COND alters whether a lock is taken, never object lifetime -> zero new UAF surface; deferred-worker nvlfp lifetime stays protected by is_accepting_opens + open_q stop/flush (E4). rm_get_adapter_status does NOT call any A8 state transition / nv_tb_egpu_f40b_fired (those are in the A6/A7 timeout branches at nv.c:1923/2342), so it cannot strand tb_egpu_state. A10-v2 (task #293, edits kernel_gsp_gh100.c + an additive nv_state_t field) is file/hunk-disjoint from A11; land A10/A10-v2 first, then A11.

DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH D1 (now PROMOTED to recommended primary-or-co-primary, reviewer #1 suggestion accepted) — kernel-open early-bail that breaks E1 WITHOUT depending on the conditional-acquire primitive. SITE: nv_open_device_for_nvlfp, nv.c:1785-1788 (the else arm). Sketch: before calling rm_get_adapter_status_external, if nv_check_gpu_state(nv)==NV_ERR_GPU_IS_LOST (existing pattern at nv.c:1698-1703) OR os_pci_is_disconnected OR the C5 sink already marks the GPU lost, set nvlfp->adapter_status = NV_ERR_GPU_IS_LOST and SKIP the external call (and thus the whole RM-API round-trip) entirely. This covers the COMMON F45 case (cold-init already declared the GPU lost) with a kernel-open-only change, improving sovereignty posture and not touching any deep-RM lock. RECOMMENDATION: ship D1 as a co-primary alongside Part 1; D1 alone breaks E1 in the already-lost case, the Part-2 COND covers the rarer transient-contention-but-not-yet-lost case. If for any reason Part 1 (primitive fix) is deferred, D1 is the primitive-INDEPENDENT cut and becomes the primary F45 fix on its own.

DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH D2 (OPTIONAL, DEFER — reviewer #3 ref correction applied) — bound the removal-arm open_q flush so a future unknown stuck-worker class cannot wedge pciehp. The flush is reached via nv_linux_stop_open_q (nv-pci.c:2451) -> nv_kthread_q_stop -> nv_kthread_q_flush -> _raw_q_flush -> wait_for_completion with NO timeout (nv-kthread-q.c:289/301). (Note: the original design's 'nv_pci_remove_helper calls nv_kthread_q_flush at 2451' was imprecise — 2451 calls the stop wrapper; a bare nv_kthread_q_flush(&open_q) exists only on the PM-suspend path at nv.c:4898.) A bounded eGPU-gated variant (wait_for_completion_timeout + WARN) would let removal proceed even if a worker wedged. NOT required for F45 (Part 1 + Part 2/D1 already prevent the worker from wedging); touches shared nv-kthread-q semantics (broader blast radius, risk of a detached in-flight item). Do NOT bundle with A11; defer unless a second independent worker-stall class is observed.

RESIDUAL RISK (must be stated): (1) adapter_status becomes best-effort under contention on the eGPU — already effectively true (default NV_ERR_OPERATING_SYSTEM) and consumed only as a userspace diagnostic (nv.c:2990-2991, escape.c:779), never an internal control gate. LOW. (2) The fix breaks the deadlock but does NOT fix the stochastic H16 cold-init failure — a transient still strands the chip lost (unusable until re-probe); A11 is a DEADLOCK-BREAKER, NOT a bringup-reliability fix. Make the catalog explicit so a future reader does not assume cold-init is now robust; cold-bringup bounded-retry is a separate task. (3) UNNAMED HOLDER: if the true permanent holder is a 5th uncaptured thread (not transient writer-starvation), cutting E1 frees 169991/151 but 254369 and 251420's post-complete blocking cleanup acquire (osapi.c:3110) remain wedged -> host still needs reboot. The fix is robust to holder IDENTITY only in the sense that the worker leaves contention; it is NOT robust to a genuinely PERMANENT holder. This is the central unconfirmable point given no vmcore.
openQuestions: PRIMITIVE-FIX SEQUENCING (now the gating decision): Part 1 (invert os_cond_acquire_rwlock_{read,write} in os-interface.c) MUST land before Part 2 (A11) because A11 inherits the corruption otherwise. It ALSO retroactively changes C1/A10 runtime behavior (today C1 either leaks the API lock on success or releases an unheld lock on contention). Human decision: ship Part 1 as a standalone foundational patch (e.g. A10.1 / C-series 'cond-acquire-primitive-correctness') WITH its own intent doc and a fresh audit confirming the only rwsem-COND consumers are rmapi.c:595 + osapi.c:1913, OR fold it into the A11 addon? Recommend standalone (it is a correctness fix to a shared primitive, not eGPU-scoped). Either way it needs a real `make modules` compile, not apply-check.

POSITIVE-ASSERTION VALIDATION (the test that would have caught the inversion): after a COND acquire reports NV_OK, assert rmapiLockIsOwner()==true (helper already exists, used at kernel_gsp.c ~5064); after TIMEOUT_RETRY, assert rmapiLockIsOwner()==false. Before relying on Part 1, confirm this assertion pair PASSES on the patched build (a tiny kernel test module that init_rwsem + down_write_trylock twice, or drgn reading g_RmApiLock.threadId/owner after an instrumented COND acquire on the live patched host). This is read-only/safe and is the empirical proof the primitive fix is correct. Should this assertion pair be added as a permanent debug-build gate?

UNNAMED LOCK HOLDER (genuinely unresolved, fix-robustness-limiting): no captured thread and not the cold-init thread holds the write lock the two writers block on, so either a 5th uncaptured R-state RM thread holds it permanently, or it is transient writer-fairness starvation made permanent by the unkillable kthread. The keystone cut is sufficient ONLY if the holder was transient. Resolving this requires a future live datapoint: on the next F45 occurrence (or the fake-5090 substrate task #290), capture FULL sysrq-t over netconsole/serial (NOT kdump — the capture kernel hangs re-probing the wedged eGPU per the RCA) and/or drgn-read g_RmApiLock.threadId to map the holder PID. Human decision: install drgn on obpc + fix/replace the kdump capture path (netconsole or a capture kernel with the eGPU blacklisted) so the next occurrence can confirm the holder.

GATE CHOICE (eGPU-gated vs unconditional COND at osapi.c:2752): gated (pNv->is_external_gpu) is the conservative A6/A7-discipline default and confines the escape.c:779 ioctl behavior change to eGPU only; unconditional is simpler and defensible (function is best-effort for all GPUs) but changes the NV_ESC_STATUS_CODE ioctl for ALL GPUs. Recommend GATED. The gate's reliability is CONFIRMED (A9 sets is_external_gpu at probe, nv-pci.c:2042, before any open) so the no-op sub-concern is closed — but reviewers must sign off on the gated-vs-unconditional policy choice.

DEFERRED-PATH PRECONDITION for the 251420<->169991 edge: confirm pid 251420's engage fd genuinely took the DEFERRED open path (required for the E2 edge). If it was a FOREGROUND open, its open_complete already fired and the close wait is on a different fd — which changes the E2 edge but NOT the keystone-cut argument. Verify the injector's `nvidia-smi -pm 1` open pattern (O_NONBLOCK usage / fd count) selects the deferred path. Also cross-check via objdump/addr2line on the deployed apnex module that nvidia_close+0x56 = the wait_for_completion in nv_wait_open_complete (nv.c:2690), and that the non-interruptible re-wait (nv.c:2661) is what makes SIGKILL ineffective.

C1/A10 RETRO-AUDIT + RE-SOAK: because Part 1 changes C1/A10's actual runtime behavior (it was previously corrupt-or-no-op), the F44 mitigation effectively has NOT been validated in its intended form. After Part 1, F44 should be re-exercised (fake-5090 lockdown substrate task #290, or a controlled obpc repro) to confirm C1 now does what its 2026-05 intent doc claims. Human decision: does this re-open the F44 validation status, and should the A10 intent doc be amended to note it depended on a then-broken primitive?
